books.title: Cane
books.author: Jean Toomer
books.summary: An experimental and highly lyrical novel exploring the African-American experience.

books.title: An Antarctic Mystery
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: An American naturalist travels south beyond the remote Kerguelen Islands.

books.title: Our Nig
books.author: Harriet E. Wilson
books.summary: An autobiographical account of a young biracial girl abandoned by her parents and forced into servitude in the northern United States.

books.title: Personal Recollections of Joan of Arc
books.author: Mark Twain
books.summary: A fictional biography of Joan of Arc from her early teenage years to her martyrdom.

books.title: The Phoenix and the Carpet
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Five children once again encounter an ancient, magical creature and go on adventures around the world with its magic carpet.

books.title: Leave It to Psmith
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: Psmith inveigles himself into Blandings Castle to help out a friend and to pursue love.

books.title: You Never Can Tell
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: A family recently returned to England navigates a comedy of errors when they accidentally meet their estranged husband and unknown father.

books.title: Harding’s Luck
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: A poor orphan boy accidentally discovers magic which sends him into the past and leads to his recovery of a fortune and a lordly title.

books.title: Marius the Epicurean
books.author: Walter Pater
books.summary: A young man in the second-century Roman empire explores philosophy and religion as he travels through life.

books.title: At the Mountains of Madness
books.author: H. P. Lovecraft
books.summary: An Antarctic expedition reveals the horrifying reality of ancient myths in the depths of the continent.

books.title: The Worst Journey in the World
books.author: Apsley Cherry-Garrard
books.summary: The travelogue of a member of Robert Scott’s ill-fated Antarctic expedition of 1910.

books.title: The Tower Treasure
books.author: Franklin W. Dixon
books.summary: After a tower mansion is robbed, two boys are determined to find the culprit and exonerate their friend’s father.

books.title: Democracy and Social Ethics
books.author: Jane Addams
books.summary: Jane Addams argues that addressing social issues like poverty and inequality requires shifting from individual morality to a collective understanding of social responsibility.

books.title: The Kural
books.author: Thiruvalluvar
books.summary: An Indian weaver-philosopher expounds on the subjects of righteousness, wealth, and love.

books.title: The Rubáiyát of Omar Khayyám
books.author: Omar Khayyám
books.summary: Three volumes of lyrical quatrains about the joys of living, the meaning of existence, and what it means to lead a happy life.

books.title: Penguin Island
books.author: Anatole France
books.summary: A myopic saint mistakenly baptizes some penguins, ushering in a centuries-spanning satire of French history.

books.title: Herland
books.author: Charlotte Perkins Gilman
books.summary: Three male explorers trek into a mysterious civilization populated entirely by women.

books.title: Conan Stories
books.author: Robert E. Howard
books.summary: A collection of Robert E. Howard’s short stories featuring Conan the Barbarian.

books.title: The Everlasting Man
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: G. K. Chesterton takes us through the history of man, rebuking common secular views and presenting the case for the Catholic Church.

books.title: Journals
books.author: Alexander Mackenzie
books.summary: Explorer Alexander Mackenzie details his journeys to both the Arctic and Pacific Oceans in his personal journals.
